name: Octalysis Framework
overview: Octalysis is a human-focused gamification design framework formalized by Yu-kai Chou (book Actionable Gamification, 2015) that maps human motivation onto 8 Core Drives arranged in an octagon. The horizontal axis splits Left Brain (extrinsic, possessions, logic) from Right Brain (intrinsic, creativity, social). The vertical axis splits White Hat (top: Epic Meaning, Accomplishment, Empowerment) from Black Hat (bottom: Scarcity, Unpredictability, Loss & Avoidance). White Hat creates fulfillment; Black Hat creates urgency/anxiety. Most engagement-only designs over-rely on Black Hat (PBL: points, badges, leaderboards) and burn users out. Octalysis is widely cited (3,400+ academic papers) but has limited rigorous RCT validation.
originator_or_company: Yu-kai Chou (Taiwanese-American gamification designer, b.1981; Octalysis Group founder; UCLA economics; advisor to Google, LEGO, eBay, Tesla, US Navy). Framework first published on yukaichou.com 2013, formalized in book Actionable Gamification: Beyond Points, Badges, and Leaderboards (Octalysis Media, May 2015). Octalysis Group (consulting arm) founded by Joris Beerda + Erik van Mechelen.
key_resources: [White Hat vs Black Hat Gamification in the Octalysis Framework - Yu-kai Chou](https://yukaichou.com/gamification-study/white-hat-black-hat-gamification-octalysis-framework/)
[Level II and III Octalysis: Advanced Framework - Yu-kai Chou](https://yukaichou.com/gamification-study/level-ii-iii-octalysis-advanced-framework/)
[The Octalysis Framework - Octalysis Group Behavioral Design Methodology](https://octalysisgroup.com/framework/)
[Actionable Gamification: Beyond Points, Badges, and Leaderboards (Chou, 2015) - Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/Actionable-Gamification-Beyond-Points-Leaderboards/dp/1511744049)
[A bibliometric analysis of the Gamification Octalysis Framework in training - Humanities and Social Sciences Communications, Nature (2023)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41599-023-02243-3)

[A game-based learning system based on octalysis gamification framework to promote employees' Japanese learning - Computers & Education (Elsevier 2023)](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0360131523001768)
[Reflection on the Octalysis framework as a design and evaluation tool - CEUR Workshop Proceedings Vol-3147](https://ceur-ws.org/Vol-3147/paper8.pdf)
core_mechanics:
Octalysis arranges 8 Core Drives on an octagon. (1) EPIC MEANING & CALLING (top, White Hat, RB): user believes they are part of something bigger or 'chosen'. Examples: Wikipedia editing, free language education for the world. (2) DEVELOPMENT & ACCOMPLISHMENT (top, White Hat, LB): the drive most exploited by PBL — progress, mastery, overcoming challenges. 'A badge or trophy without a challenge is meaningless.' (3) EMPOWERMENT OF CREATIVITY & FEEDBACK (top, White Hat, RB): repeated experimentation with visible results — Lego, painting, sandbox modes. (4) OWNERSHIP & POSSESSION (middle, LB): personalized avatars, accumulated assets, customization, virtual currency. (5) SOCIAL INFLUENCE & RELATEDNESS (middle, RB): mentorship, comparison, friendship, competition, nostalgia. (6) SCARCITY & IMPATIENCE (bottom, Black Hat, LB): exclusivity, appointment dynamics, drip-feed unlocks. (7) UNPREDICTABILITY & CURIOSITY (bottom, Black Hat, RB): variable rewards, mystery boxes, sweepstakes — same drive that powers gambling. (8) LOSS & AVOIDANCE (bottom, Black Hat, LB): fear of losing streaks, FOMO, fading opportunities. 

FOUR LEVELS: Level I = static snapshot of which Core Drives are present. Level II = overlay 4 EXPERIENCE PHASES (Discovery -> Onboarding -> Scaffolding -> Endgame; same Core Drive lands differently in each phase). Level III = overlay PLAYER TYPES (Achiever / Socializer / Free Spirit / Philanthropist analogs) and ethical White/Black Hat tuning per type. Levels IV-V are organizational rollout. 

DESIGN HEURISTIC: White Hat alone has no urgency ('I will save the world... after breakfast'); pure Black Hat causes burnout (Zynga case). Healthy products mix both, dialing Black Hat down as users transition from Onboarding to Endgame.
designer_or_researcher_voice: Yu-kai Chou: 'White Hat Core Drives are motivation elements that make us feel powerful, fulfilled, and satisfied. Black Hat Core Drives make us feel obsessed, anxious, and addicted.' (yukaichou.com, White Hat vs Black Hat post)
Yu-kai Chou: 'If there aren't any Black Hat techniques, there likely won't be any breakout success; if there aren't any White Hat techniques, users quickly burnout.' (Actionable Gamification, 2015)

Yu-kai Chou: 'Just because something is called Black Hat Gamification doesn't mean it's necessarily bad or unethical' — citing the SnuzNLuz alarm clock that donates to your most-hated charity if you oversleep, leveraging Loss & Avoidance for the user's own benefit. (yukaichou.com)

Yu-kai Chou on PBL: 'Most companies just slap on Points, Badges and Leaderboards (PBL) and call it gamification. That's the equivalent of putting frosting on cardboard. Octalysis asks: which human drives are active here, and which ones are missing?' (paraphrased from yukaichou.com / Actionable Gamification preface) [uncertain]

Yu-kai Chou on the Zynga collapse: '[Zynga's] stock crashed 80% and they laid off 520 employees because their games lacked Core Drive 3 (Empowerment of Creativity & Feedback) — they were almost pure Black Hat scarcity and loss aversion.' (paraphrased from Actionable Gamification ch.4) [uncertain]
metrics_or_evidence: Adoption: book sold 100,000+ copies, cited in 3,400+ PhD theses and academic journals (Yu-kai Chou self-reported). Used by Google, LEGO, Tesla, eBay, Microsoft, Volkswagen, Huawei, IDEO; Octalysis Group claims 1.5B end-users impacted. Empirical study: a 2023 Computers & Education paper (Wu et al., game-based Japanese learning system using all 8 Core Drives) reported significant gains in learner engagement, motivation, immersion, and performance vs control; intrinsic-motivation drives correlated with higher learning outcomes. EFL classroom studies (7th-grade English in Indonesia, 2024) show qualitative engagement uplift. However, the 2023 Nature HSSC bibliometric review notes 'empirical and theoretical challenges remain in demonstrating the effectiveness of the framework and codifying the principles underpinning effective gamification design.' [uncertain on exact effect sizes — most evidence is case-study or quasi-experimental, not RCT.]
criticisms: (1) Limited rigorous validation: the 2023 Nature HSSC bibliometric analysis explicitly flags 'empirical and theoretical challenges' and notes that 'only a few academic articles have reflected on its applicability'. Most published work is case study, not RCT. (2) Promotional/self-published roots: the framework lives mainly on Chou's blog and self-published 2015 book; Wikipedia's Octalysis article carries a 'contains promotional content' notice. (3) Construct overlap: the 8 Core Drives are not psychometrically validated and overlap with established theories (Self-Determination Theory's autonomy/competence/relatedness; Bartle's player types; Reiss's 16 motives). (4) Subjectivity: scoring an experience on the Octalysis octagon is rater-dependent; no inter-rater reliability published. (5) Black Hat over-reliance risk: practitioners often gravitate to Black Hat drives (scarcity, loss aversion, variable rewards) because they show short-term metric lifts — same Overjustification / dopamine-loop critique that hits Skinner-box gamification generally. (6) Cultural generalizability: drives like Epic Meaning are rooted in Western individualist framing; Asian collectivist contexts may weight Social Influence/Relatedness differently.
failure_cases: (1) Zynga (FarmVille / CityVille) - Chou's flagship cautionary tale: pure Black Hat scarcity + appointment dynamics + loss aversion produced explosive growth then collapse — stock down 80% (2012), 520 layoffs, Core Drive 3 (Empowerment & Feedback) absent. (2) Generic PBL (points/badges/leaderboards) bolt-ons in enterprise LMS: practitioners cite Octalysis to diagnose why these failed — they hit Drive 2 only, ignore Drives 1, 3, 5. (3) Loot boxes / gacha mechanics in mobile games: reliance on Drive 7 (Unpredictability) without White Hat balance has triggered Belgium / Netherlands gambling regulation and Apple App Store kid-game scrutiny. (4) Foursquare's pre-2014 mayorship/badge era — strong on Drive 4 (Ownership), thin on Drives 1, 3 — engagement collapsed when novelty (Drive 7) wore off; the company pivoted to Swarm and lost the gamification leadership narrative. [uncertain on attribution of these to Octalysis specifically — some are post-hoc analyses by gamification consultants]
antipatterns: (1) PBL bolt-on: stamping points, badges, leaderboards onto a service without auditing all 8 drives — hits only Drive 2, leaves the user-experience hollow. (2) Black Hat stacking: layering Scarcity + Unpredictability + Loss together (e.g. limited-time loot boxes that decay if you skip a day) — produces compulsion and burnout, regulator attention under EU DSA dark-pattern rules. (3) Means-Ends Reversal: users chase the gamified metric (badge, streak count, league rank) rather than the underlying skill/learning. (4) Endgame neglect: driving Discovery / Onboarding hard with extrinsic rewards (Drives 2, 4, 6) but offering no Drive 1 (Epic Meaning) or Drive 3 (Creative Empowerment) for veterans — users leave once novelty fades. (5) One-size-fits-all: ignoring Level III player-type tuning; e.g. forcing leaderboards (Drive 5 competitive) on a Free Spirit / Philanthropist user who finds them stressful. (6) Treating Black Hat as inherently unethical and removing it entirely — produces a feel-good but inert product (no urgency = no behavior change).
applicability_to_shikaq: Strong fit as the *meta-framework* for shikaq's incentive design audit. The 20s-female cafe-iPad persona is largely White-Hat-leaning (Free Spirit + Achiever HEXAD types per Marczewski mapping) and reacts negatively to heavy Black Hat (cartoon mascot guilt, gacha boxes, public leaderboards) — Apple HIG aesthetics already enforce this. Translation playbook per drive: (1) EPIC MEANING -> 'why this qualification matters' onboarding narrative, founder letter, success-story shelf. Light, opt-in. (2) DEVELOPMENT & ACCOMPLISHMENT -> Apple-Fitness-style 3-ring progress (questions correct, study minutes, stretch goal); SF Symbol checkmark.seal.fill milestones. THE primary lever for shikaq Phase 1. (3) EMPOWERMENT & FEEDBACK -> in-app Playground for FE Kamoku-B (run code, see results), AI Q&A on user notes, 'try a harder variant' button. (4) OWNERSHIP -> custom avatars, study-plan personalization (chosen qualification, daily goal slider), saved bookmarks, cumulative correct-answer count framed as 'your study log'. (5) SOCIAL INFLUENCE -> opt-in Family Plan / 仲間 mode (peers, not strangers), iMessage rich-link streak share. Avoid public leaderboards by default (Japan 20s-female persona research and Pokemon Sleep / Apple Fitness Japan benchmarks show private rings outperform public ranks). (6) SCARCITY -> use sparingly: 'today's 5 morning questions' is appointment dynamic; avoid time-limited paywall offers. (7) UNPREDICTABILITY -> variable AI feedback wording, surprise audio-quiz selection at night; NO chest/gacha mechanics. (8) LOSS & AVOIDANCE -> a calm continuity ring (not a fire emoji) with built-in free Streak Freeze/'rest day', framed as health rather than punishment. HEXAD mapping: Achiever (Drive 2), Free Spirit (Drives 3, 4), Philanthropist (Drive 1), Socializer (Drive 5 opt-in). Disruptor and Player avoided.
phase_assignment: Phase 1 (3 weeks) - meta-framework only, used as audit lens for the existing Today/Practice/Summary screens to identify which Core Drives are present/missing. Concrete Phase 1 mechanic: Drive 2 Accomplishment (Today rings) + Drive 8 calm Loss Avoidance (continuity ring with free freeze). Phase 2 (4-6 weeks): add Drive 3 Empowerment (Playground for FE Kamoku-B, AI feedback variants) and Drive 4 Ownership (custom goal, study-plan personalization). Phase 3 (6-8 weeks): add Drive 1 Epic Meaning (success-story shelf, founder narrative), Drive 5 Social (opt-in 仲間 mode), Drive 6 light Scarcity (morning 5-Q appointment), Drive 7 light Unpredictability (variable AI praise, audio-quiz surprise). Rationale: Octalysis is not a buildable feature — it is the diagnostic that decides which features get built. Apply Level I in Phase 1, Level II (phase overlay) in Phase 2, Level III (player-type tuning) in Phase 3.
integration_points: No DB tables or runtime code. Delivered as an internal design-review artifact: (a) `.claude/rules/octalysis-audit.md` — 8-drive checklist applied to each shikaq screen (Today / Learn / Practice / Summary / AIQA / Audio / Playground / Bookmarks). (b) Octalysis octagon rendered as a section in `incentive_ui_design/outline.yaml` outputs. (c) Quarterly design review where each PR touching incentive surfaces (Sidebar streak indicator, TodayContent rings, Practice question feedback, Summary streak history) is scored against the 8 drives + 4 phases. (d) HEXAD player-type mapping doc that ties Octalysis Level III to the existing user persona and informs default toggles (e.g. leagues default OFF). (e) Optional: an admin-only screen in the shikaq admin panel that surfaces an Octalysis score per content section to spot Drive 1 / Drive 3 deficits.
risks: (1) Framework-as-decoration: teams cite Octalysis but never run a real audit — risk of treating the octagon as a marketing diagram. Mitigate with concrete `.claude/rules/octalysis-audit.md` checklist and per-PR review. (2) Over-engineering: applying Level III player-types prematurely (before Phase 1 ships) causes scope creep. (3) Misuse to justify Black Hat: Chou's 'Black Hat is not necessarily unethical' line can be weaponized to greenlight scarcity/FOMO patterns that conflict with Apple HIG and Center for Humane Technology guidance. shikaq must add an explicit 'Black Hat ethics check' that asks: does this serve the *user's* stated goal (passing IT Pass / FE / SPI / Boki2) or the *company's* DAU goal? (4) Cultural calibration: Drive 1 Epic Meaning copywriting tuned for US individualism doesn't land on Japanese 20s-female persona; rewrite as 'カフェで参考書を広げる代わりに、iPad で 1 問' (concrete, intimate, not heroic). (5) Evidence weakness: the framework's empirical base is thin — do not cite Octalysis to leadership as if it were RCT-validated. Pair with Self-Determination Theory and HEXAD where stronger evidence exists.
apple_hig_compatibility: compatible (as a meta-framework). Octalysis is a diagnostic lens, not a visual style, so it imposes no UI on shikaq. Its White Hat lean (Drives 1, 2, 3) aligns naturally with Apple HIG's Clarity / Deference / Less-but-better and Apple Fitness's intrinsic-motivation framing. The risk is that *implementations* of Octalysis drives (cartoon badges, fire-streak emojis, gacha unlocks, public leagues) violate HIG — but those are implementation choices, not the framework itself. shikaq must filter every Octalysis-driven feature through the existing design-principles.md gate (SF Pro, SF Symbols, semantic colors, Liquid Glass tabbar, calm second-person copy).
ethical_score: neutral. Octalysis itself is ethics-agnostic — it is a vocabulary for talking about motivation, and explicitly distinguishes White Hat (positive) from Black Hat (urgency/anxiety). Center for Humane Technology framing maps cleanly onto White Hat. The score depends on which drives the implementer emphasizes: a White-Hat-only shikaq design is positive (mastery, autonomy, purpose); a Black-Hat-heavy implementation (Scarcity + Unpredictability + Loss stacked) is the same dark-pattern compulsion stack flagged for Duolingo / loot-box mobile games and would be net-negative. shikaq's Apple HIG / 20s-female persona constraints already push toward White Hat, so adopting Octalysis as the audit lens is net-positive provided the Black Hat ethics check (Risk #3 above) is enforced.
evidence_quality: theoretical (with company_case_study supporting). The framework is a synthesis of behavioral-psych concepts (intrinsic/extrinsic motivation, loss aversion, variable rewards) into a designer-facing taxonomy. Validation is mostly case study (Microsoft Learn, GJLS Japanese-learning, EFL classrooms) and bibliometric (Nature HSSC 2023 review). No RCTs of 'Octalysis-designed product vs control' at scale. Underlying components (loss aversion, variable rewards, autonomy) are well-validated independently in academic literature; Octalysis's contribution is taxonomic and pedagogic, not empirical.
localization_fit_japan: high (as meta-framework) / needs_adaptation (per drive). The 8 Core Drives are largely culture-general, but weighting must shift for the 20代女性キャリア層 persona: Drive 1 Epic Meaning lands less as 'change the world' and more as 'become the version of yourself who passes 簿記2 / FE'; Drive 5 Social Influence works as 仲間 (peer support) rather than 競争 (public ranking) — Pokemon Sleep, Apple Fitness Japan, NHK ラジオ講座 and Studyplus benchmark data all confirm Japanese learners prefer private continuity over public leaderboards. Drive 6 Scarcity (limited-time offers, FOMO countdowns) is culturally noisier in Japan and more likely to read as 押し売り; use sparingly. Drive 8 Loss Avoidance via streak/continuity has strong cultural fit (継続は力なり / 続ける文化) — but framing must be calm (Apple Fitness ring), not guilt-inducing (Duolingo sad owl). The framework's Western-individualist origins are surmountable with localization, not blocking.
